message: From an Occupational Therapy standpoint, the pacing budget ring needs to change color before 100%, not at - participants who are already symptomatic need the warning earlier. Suggest threshold at 75% yellow, 90% orange.
Good catch @fschultz-Occupational Therapy - adjusting thresholds to 75/90. Will push updated version tomorrow.
The 0-10 slider looks great but professionally we should label 0 = no pain and 10 = worst imaginable pain, not just numbers. This matches the NRS standard language my participants already know.
Updated! Added endpoint labels per Frank's feedback. Resolving.

Step 4 asks about pain topic before asking about activity baseline - professionally this is backwards. Readers anchor to topic too early, which biases their activity estimates. Recommend swapping steps 4 and 5.
Agree - Frank's feedback here is exactly the kind of thing we need professional input for. Willa let's discuss in Thursday's design sync.

The Occupational Therapy community partner portal looks solid. One request: can we add a field for 'primary topic category' that maps to our ICD-10 groupings? It would let me filter participants by condition type quickly.

The 7-day sparkline is a great start but I'd want to see a 30-day trend view too - in chronic pain group Occupational Therapy we're tracking monthly trends, not just weekly. Could this be a toggle on the chart?
Adding 30-day toggle to the backlog. @fschultz-Occupational Therapy thanks for the professional framing - this will also help for trainer users who track post-surgical recovery over months.

Flagging from Occupational Therapy lens: many chronic pain group participants have fatigue and cognitive load issues. The activity log entry flow is 6 taps - we should aim for 3 or fewer for the most common daily entry. Reducing friction = better adherence.

The home screen is clean. The 'How are you feeling today?' prompt is great - but consider anchoring it to morning vs afternoon, since symptom patterns differ by time of day and that context matters for pacing.

The message thread design is good. I'd strongly recommend the coach view shows the last 7 days of pacing data alongside the message - so OTs aren't switching contexts to look up chart data when composing a message.

The progress report template looks professional. For Occupational Therapy documentation purposes, can we add a section for 'Goals of Care' and 'Functional Outcomes'? Those are required for insurance billing narrative.
New version is live - pacing ring thresholds updated per Frank's professional guidance. Please review when you get a chance @fschultz-Occupational Therapy.
Users should be able to set their 'typical good day' as a baseline in settings - not just their topic. Pacing is relative to personal capacity, not an absolute scale.

The overdoing alert is good but the language 'You've exceeded your limit' sounds punitive. chronic pain group participants respond poorly to shame-adjacent framing. Suggest 'Heads up - you're approaching your pacing limit for today.'
Yes! Updating copy throughout. Frank's right - participant-centered language is non-negotiable here. Resolving.

The Alert Banner 'critical' state uses all red - consider that ~8% of males have red-green color deficiency. Would recommend adding an icon (warning triangle) to not rely on color alone. This is also a WCAG 1.4.1 requirement.

The advisor onboarding guide is clear. One addition: page 3 should include the privacy BAA reminder - Occupational Therapy advisors need to know they're covered by the BAA before they look at any de-identified aggregate data.

Dark mode looks great. Worth noting - many participants with chronic pain group or fatigue use their phones at night and dark mode matters a lot to them. This is a high-value feature from an adherence standpoint.
